english: 1. the letter w, which occurs but rarely, and only as an initial, yet it is a true Tibetan letter, the Ssk. व being gen. represented by བ, and as second constituent of a double consonant denoted by ྭ (called wa-zúr (wa zur) angular or small wa); the pronunciation in general is the same as that of the English w. — 2. num. fig.: 20.

1. water-channel, gutter, gen. of wood (Cs. also: trough); wa-k'a (wa kha) id., Cs.; wa-mcu (wa mchu) spout, lip, or beak of vessels. — 2. fox (the name corresponding to the sound of barking) Dzl., vulgo wa-tsé (wa tse); wa brgyal (wa brgyal) the fox yelps Sch. The fox is the riding-beast of the goblins; whenever his barking is heard, it is in consequence of his receiving lashes from his rider. — wa-skyés (wa skyes) fox-born Cs. — wa-gró (wa gro) a bluish fox, gro-gró (gro gro) a gray fox Sch. — wa-rgán (wa rgan) an old fox, a knave Cs. — wa-ldéb (wa ldeb) fox-trap W. — wa-nág (wa nag) a blackish fox Sch. — wa-lpágs (wa lpags) fox's skin. — wa-spyaṅ (wa spyang) Mil., wá-ma-spyaṅ (wa ma spyang) Cs. jackall. — wa-prug (wa phrug) young fox, cub. — wá-mo (wa mo) she-fox. — wa-tsaṅ (wa tshang) fox-hole. — wa-róg (wa rog) black fox Sch. — wa-tswá (wa tshwa) a kind of salt S.g. — 3. n. of a lunar mansion, v. rgyu-skár (rgyu skar). — 4. wa-lóg-pa (wa log pa) to perform somersets Sch. — 5. W. ho! calling for one.

wylie: wA ra Na si, or se, ba ra Na si
english: Banaras, a city in the valley of the Ganges, frq mentioned in legends, as a residence of Buddha, at the present time a principal seat of Brahmanism.

wylie: wa le, wal le, wal le ba
english: clear, distinct, plain, wa-lér drán-pa (wa ler dran pa) to recollect distinctly Cs.; yid-la (yid la) floating distinctly before one's mind Lex.; don wa-lé gyis (don wa le gyis) try to gain a clear understanding of the sense of it Mil.; also skad-wál (skad wal) = श्राशव्द(?).

1. a letter of the alphabet, represented by ż, originally, and in the frontier-provinces to the present day, the soft sibilant, which is pronounced like j in French, or like the English s in leisure, (zh), (still more accurately like the Polish z in zima); in C. it differs now from ཤ only by the following vowel being deep-toned. — 2. numerical figure: 21.

resp. dbu-żwa (dbu zhwa), a covering of the head, hat, cap; fig. ṅa yíg sá-yi żwá-ċan (nga yig sa yi zhwa can) the letter ང having ས for a cap: སྔ Zam.; żwa gón-pa (zhwa gon pa), gyón-pa (gyon pa) to put the cap on, búd-pa (bud pa) to take it off
